title: Evaluate retrieval quality
description: Use a labeled query-passage set to measure recall, precision, and grounding, separating retrieval failures from generation failures.
body: Steps: 1. Build a labeled set of queries with their relevant passage ids. 2. Measure first-stage recall and rerank precision against the labels. 3. Measure answer grounding: does each claim trace to a retrieved passage. 4. Attribute each failure to retrieval (missing passage) or generation (passage present but ignored). 5. Track these metrics as a regression suite. Rollback point: before tuning chunking, embeddings, or rerank. Validation: failures are correctly attributed and a change that lowers recall is caught by the suite.
